primary_sources.notes: India IT-BPM industry: $254B revenue FY25. Average employee cost to revenue 58-62%, subcontracting 6-10%.
Revenue ₹246,108 Cr. Employee benefit expenses ₹128,866 Cr (52.4% of revenue). Subcontracting ₹12,300 Cr (5.0%).
Revenue ₹162,990 Cr. Employee benefit expenses ₹94,891 Cr (58.2%). Subcontracting ₹14,900 Cr (9.1%). Travel ₹2,940 Cr (1.8%).
IT Services revenue ~₹88,456 Cr. Employee benefits ~₹54,843 Cr (62.0%). Subcontracting ~₹6,800 Cr (7.7%).
Revenue ~₹115,000 Cr. Employee costs ~₹68,000 Cr (59.1%). Subcontracting ~₹10,350 Cr (9.0%).

primary_sources.source_name: Gartner IT Key Metrics Data: IT Budget Benchmarks
primary_sources.notes: IT services firm internal IT spend benchmarks. IT infra 2-4% of revenue for services firms.

source_metadata.methodology: P25/P50/P75 derived from FY2024-25 annual reports of 5 listed Indian IT companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL, LTIMindtree) plus Mphasis as P90 anchor. NASSCOM industry survey used for cross-validation. P25 is best-in-class (lowest spend = highest efficiency). Sample of 6 companies represents ~80% of listed Indian IT services revenue.

HR.source_note: Employee benefit expense % of revenue. TCS FY25: 52.4%, Infosys: 58.2%, HCL: 59.1%, LTIMindtree: 60.8%, Wipro: 62.0%, Mphasis: 67.1%. Sorted: 52.4→58.2→59.1→60.8→62.0→67.1 giving P25≈52, P50≈59, P75≈63, P90≈70 (adjusted for industry distribution).

OUTSOURCED.source_note: Third-party subcontracting / external delivery. TCS FY25: 5.0%, Wipro: 7.7%, HCL: 9.0%, Infosys: 9.1%. NASSCOM notes 10-15% subcon at high-growth ITES firms.

IT.source_note: Internal IT infrastructure, software licences. Gartner IT Key Metrics 2024: IT services firms spend 2-4% of revenue on internal IT. Best-in-class near 1.5% (shared infra, high cloud discipline).

TRAVEL.source_note: Travel & entertainment. Infosys FY25: 1.8%, Wipro FY25: ~2.2%. Industry averaged 1.5-2.5%. NASSCOM: post-COVID T&E stabilising at ~2% of revenue.

FACILITIES.source_note: Rent & facilities. Infosys campus model shows lower rent (Ind AS 116 lease-adjusted). HCL SEZ footprint ~1.8%. NASSCOM: Tier-1 city campus firms 1.5-2%, leased office firms 2.5-3.5%.

PROF_SVCS.source_note: Legal, audit, external consulting. INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — calibrated from Indian IT sector compliance and M&A advisory spend patterns.

MARKETING.source_note: Sales & marketing. TCS: ~1.0%, Infosys: ~1.4% (includes analyst relations, brand). Wipro higher due to cloud go-to-market push ~2.0%. Mid-tier ITES ~2.5-3.0%.

TELECOM.source_note: Communications and network costs. INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — estimated from India IT firm connectivity spend (MPLS, internet, collaboration tools).

INSURANCE.source_note: Group health insurance, D&O, professional indemnity. INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — calibrated from Indian IT sector statutory benefit structures and E&O policy benchmarks.

OFFICE.source_note: Office supplies, printing, sundry admin. INTERNAL_CALIBRATION.

CONTINGENT.source_note: Contract/gig workforce. NASSCOM 2025: contract headcount 15-25% of workforce in ITES; estimated cost at market billing rates.
